overview: Dimir Lurrus Control is a Vintage strategy that leverages recursion and disruption to outlast opponents. By casting Lurrus of the Dream-Den early, the deck repeatedly plays cheap, high-impact spells like Orcish Bowmasters and Psychic Frog. This approach generates sustained card advantage and board control until the opponent runs out of resources.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents through superior card advantage and recursion
Replaying Orcish Bowmasters to remove key threats repeatedly
Using Psychic Frog to disrupt opponent casting while generating value

keep_criteria: Hands with early interaction and reliable land drops
Hands containing Lurrus of the Dream-Den with cheap recursion targets
Hands with multiple cantrips or disruption spells
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, Lurrus of the Dream-Den, and 1-2 1-drops (Orcish Bowmasters/Psychic Frog) or early interaction (Thoughtseize/Dismember).
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early disruption or land drops
Hands with only high-cost cards and no recursion
Hands missing Lurrus or its targets in a control matchup
Mulligan hands with 3+ lands, no Lurrus/1-drops, or only expensive spells that can't impact turns 1-2.

early_game: Play lands and hold up mana for disruption
Cast Lurrus of the Dream-Den as soon as possible
Use cheap interaction to delay opponent threats
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize/Dismember to disrupt, or drop a 1-drop if the board is clear.
Turn 2: Develop mana, cast Lurrus or another 1-drop, or play a removal spell.
Sequence lands to ensure consistent blue/black mana availability for interaction and Lurrus casting.
mid_game: Replay Orcish Bowmasters to remove recurring threats
Use Psychic Frog to counter high-value spells
Maintain board control while drawing into late-game answers
Preserve Lurrus as your primary engine
avoid overcommitting to the board if you can recur threats next turn.
Manage your graveyard carefully—don't fill it with non-Lurrus targets, but actively dump 1-drops and removal to fuel flashback.
Use cantrips (Ponder/Brainstorm) to dig for Lurrus or interaction when resources thin.
Commit threats when you have removal or counterspells to protect them, or when you can safely recur them next turn.
Hold back if facing sweepers or if your graveyard is empty.
late_game: Leverage Lurrus recursion to replay key answers repeatedly
Stall until opponent runs out of resources
Close out with recursive removal or counters

Lurrus of the Dream-Den replaying Orcish Bowmasters/Psychic Frog for incremental damage and targeted removal, combined with Teferi, Hero of Dominaria or The Wandering Emperor for card advantage and a reliable clock.
Go all-in when you have Lurrus on board, a full graveyard of 1-drops/removal, and enough interaction to protect your board or answer their sweepers.
Also commit when the opponent is at low life and you can recur threats for lethal before they stabilize.

key_cards.name: Lurrus of the Dream-Den
key_cards.notes: Prioritize casting early and use it to replay cheap, high-impact spells like Orcish Bowmasters and Psychic Frog to maximize value.

key_cards.name: Orcish Bowmasters
key_cards.notes: Use to eliminate key opponent threats, then replay via Lurrus to handle multiple problems or generate card advantage.

key_cards.name: Psychic Frog
key_cards.notes: Cast to force opponent to pay extra mana or counter their spells, then replay with Lurrus to disrupt multiple turns.

sequencing_tips: Hold up Lurrus recursion until you have a clear target to avoid wasting mana
Prioritize removing threats that can generate immediate board presence or card advantage
Save Psychic Frog for turns when opponent is vulnerable to mana taxes or has high-cost spells queued
Common mistakes include overfilling the graveyard with non-Lurrus targets, forgetting that Lurrus only returns 2-mana-or-less spells, and mismanaging the flashback mechanic.
Remember that Psychic Frog and Orcish Bowmasters are both 1-mana, making them ideal Lurrus targets.
Track the opponent's graveyard to avoid giving them free Lurrus targets if they run similar strategies, and always prioritize protecting Lurrus as your primary win condition engine.

matchups.advice: Focus on early disruption and replaying Orcish Bowmasters to clear boards repeatedly.
Prioritize countering key combo pieces and use Psychic Frog to disrupt mana-intensive turns.
Race for board control and use Lurrus to out-value opponent interaction.
Use cantrips to dig around counters, cast threats at the end of the opponent's turn, use flashback/removal to bypass counters, or sideboard in more uncounterable threats/sweepers.
Apply early pressure to race them, use efficient removal (Dismember, Fatal Push) on key ramp creatures, and sideboard in artifact/enchantment hate or additional removal to slow their acceleration.

Win the resource war by optimizing Lurrus targets, winning the graveyard race, and sideboarding in more card advantage (Teferi, Wandering Emperor) and counterspells. Prioritize keeping Lurrus safe and trading efficiently to outlast them.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Supreme Verdict, Anger), graveyard strategies (Dredge, Oath), and combo win conditions.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells or hold up interaction.
Track graveyard contents to anticipate Lurrus targets and avoid giving them free recursion.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Archetype name and signature cards indicate a control strategy focused on recursion and disruption in Vintage.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards suggest recursive disruption and threat removal as primary paths to victory.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: General control piloting principles applied to the provided signature cards suggest careful timing for recursion and disruption.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Vintage format dynamics and control deck vulnerabilities suggest these as typical opposing strategies.
